mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding economic output, which lifts long-run growth expectations and inflation fears, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the ensuing quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day yield change is negative in at least 6 of 10 consecutive GDP releases where the quarter-over-quarter growth rate exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 7.5% signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage and inflation expectations, which drives the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 10.0% in 2009 and has been trending down
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a UNRATE release below 7.5%, the 10-year yield declines more than 10bps in at least 3 out of 5 occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate robust domestic demand, boosting GDP growth expectations and pushing the 10-year yield upward over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When both HOUST month-over-month growth > 2% and PAYEMS growth > 150,000 occur simultaneously, the 20-day yield change is negative more often than positive over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment (FEDFUNDS near 0%), a faster-than-expected rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) stokes fears of eventual Fed tightening, causing the 10-year yield to increase sharply within 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed had held FEDFUNDS at 0-0.25% since Dec 2008
failure_condition: Following a CPIAUCSL release with m/m > 0.3%, the 5-day yield change is negative or flat in at least 5 out of 8 cases in the 2010-2013 period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer spending, which drives GDP growth and inflation expectations, leading to higher 10-year Treasury yields over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 2-year rank correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day yield change is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,000,000 units annually signals a housing market recovery, which bolsters growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After HOUST exceeds 1,000, the 20-day yield change is negative in a majority of instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) together with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic expansion, which raises long-term growth and inflation expectations, lifting the 10-year yield over the next month.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise month-over-month, the 20-day yield change is negative in at least 40% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of expected Fed monetary policy normalization (FEDFUNDS still near zero but taper talk emerging), stronger-than-expected payroll gains (PAYEMS > 200,000) are interpreted as a sign that the Fed will reduce QE sooner, sending the 10-year yield higher within 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:In May 2013, taper talk began after strong payrolls
failure_condition: Following a PAYEMS release > 200,000 during the taper regime (May 2013 onward), the 5-day yield change is negative or flat in at least 4 out of 6 subsequent events.
